From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Soviet geodetic datum

The **SK-42 reference system** is a [geodetic datum](/wiki/Geodetic_datum "Geodetic datum") established in the [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union") in 1942 as *Systema koordinat* ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Система координат 1942 года).
It was used in geodetic calculations, notably in military mapping and determining state borders.

SK-42 is based on the **Krasovsky 1940 ellipsoid**, a locally best fitting [reference ellipsoid](/wiki/Reference_ellipsoid "Reference ellipsoid") named after [Feodosy Krasovsky](/wiki/Feodosy_Krasovsky "Feodosy Krasovsky"). It has a [semi-major axis](/wiki/Semi-major_axis "Semi-major axis") (equatorial radius) *a* of 6,378,245 m, and an inverse flattening 1/*f* of 298.3 [[1]](#cite_note-mazurova-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2): 220

SK-42 served as a foundation for developing the [SK-63 reference system](/w/index.php?title=SK-63_reference_system&action=edit&redlink=1 "SK-63 reference system (page does not exist)") which was created and used primarily for civilian and industrial development purposes.[[1]](#cite_note-mazurova-1)
It was later linked to the [geocentric coordinate system](/wiki/Geocentric_coordinate_system "Geocentric coordinate system") [PZ-90](/wiki/PZ-90 "PZ-90") via a [datum transformation](/wiki/Datum_transformation "Datum transformation").[[1]](#cite_note-mazurova-1)
